sanskrit: गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान् श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके । हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
transliteration: gurūn ahatvā hi mahānubhāvān śreyo bhoktuṃ bhaikṣyam apīha loke | hatvārtha-kāmāṃs tu gurūn ihaiva bhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān ||
literal_meaning: Not having killed the great-souled teachers, it is better to enjoy alms even in this world; but having killed the teachers here itself, one would have to enjoy enjoyments stained with blood.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences a moral paralysis: he would rather live as a beggar than survive on the moral debt of killing his mentors, revealing an acute fear of ego-dystonic action that would forever contaminate his self-image.

shadow: wish to be absolved of responsibility

psychological_state: Obsessive-compulsive doubt: an ego-syntonic value (non-violence to mentors) collides with a dharma-syntonic duty (warrior code), producing intrusive images of blood-tainted rewards.
trigger_scenario: A whistle-blower asked to expose a corrupt yet beloved mentor must choose between career advancement and lifelong self-contempt.
anchor_text: Choose integrity over victory when the cost is your soul.
